name: Ría de Corme y Laxe
approachNote: WPT 43°15'·31N 09°00'·63W: track 081°/2·1M to Corme pierhd; 166°/2·0M to Lage pierhd. Appr straightforward but beware rocks off Pta del Roncudo and Pta de Laxe. In bad weather follow the ldg lines to Corme to avoid Bajo de La Averia (5-6m). On SC 4131 many depths in apprs and in the ría may be less than charted on AC 1113.
shelter: Corme: well sheltered from E and N winds, pier extended. Laxe (Lage): well sheltered from W and S; good holding/protection N of hbr bkwtr. Both hbrs may be affected by swell in winds from W–N.

name: Camariñas
approachNote: WPT 43°07'·27N 09°12'·65W (intersection of 108° and 081° ldg lines). Thence track 108°/1·1M, then 039°/0·7M to Camariñas bkwtr hd. For Mugia bkwtr 180°/0·9M. El Bufardo (43°10'·00N 09°13'·28W) a dangerous pinnacle rk awash at CD, 4ca NW of C. Villano; pass E of Las Quebrantas shoal (0·6m). From S appr on the 081° ldg lts.
shelter: Good in Camariñas, or ⊥ to N or NW of hbr except in E/NE'lies. Mugia (43°06'·36N 09°12'·73W) well sheltered in all but strong E/SE'lies either in Muxia Marina Cataventos, or ⊥ outside hbr in lee of bkwtr.

marina.name: CN Camariñas (CNC)
marina.notes: 60 + visitor berths on 3 YC pontoons, F&A on 2 pontoons; max 18m LOA; wi-fi has limited range, best near block

name: Muxia (Mugia) Marina Cataventos
approachNote: From WPT for Camariñas, take Mugia bkwtr track 180°/0·9M. Beware El Bufardo pinnacle rk awash at CD, 4ca NW of C. Villano.
shelter: Well sheltered in all but strong E/SE'lies in Muxia Marina Cataventos, or ⊥ outside hbr in lee of bkwtr.

marina.name: Muxia Marina Cataventos
marina.notes: 232 inc ⊕; €2·00, multihulls +50%; max 22m LOA; wi-fi; Town

passageNotes: CABO FINISTERRE TO THE PORTUGUESE BORDER: NE of Cabo Finisterre (AC 3674) the Ensenada del Sardineiro and Ría de Corcubión are sheltered anchorages except in S'lies and fish farms. From 5 to 11M south, beware various islets, reefs and shoals N and NW of Pta Insua, notably the unmarked Las Arrosas and Bajo de los Meixidos; the latter is in the red sector (070°-090°) of Pta Insua and can be passed inshore if bound to/from Ría de Muros. The 40M stretch (AC 3633) from Pta Queixal to Cabo Silleiro forms an impressive cruising ground encompassing the four major rías bajas (lower or southern) from N–S: Muros, Arosa, Pontevedra and Vigo, all resembling large and scenic Scottish sea lochs with interesting pilotage to fishing hbrs and many sheltered anchorages. In summer NE winds can blow strongly down all rías, usually without raising significant sea. The last three rías are sheltered from onshore winds by the coastal islands at their mouth. These Galician islands have protected National Park status, with restrictions on pleasure craft, anchoring, fishing and diving; see www.iatlanticas.es. Ría de Muros (AC 1756): smallest and easiest to enter; Monte Louro 239m conspic N side; ancient town Muros worth visiting; 212-berth Marina Muros has visitor space; RCN Portosin is a well-run modern marina. Ría de Arosa (AC 1764): largest ría, 14M inland, up to 6M wide; Villagarcia is principal hbr and marina; other marinas at Santa Uxia de Ribeira, Caramiñal, Puerto Cabo de Cruz, Rianxo, Vilanova and Piedras Negras; several active fishing fleets; night pilotage not advised; Isla Arosa and Peninsula del Grove provide intricate pilotage and secluded anchorages. Ría de Pontevedra (AC 1732): entered from NW by two minor channels; main entrance Boca del Sudoeste from SW; Sanxenxo modern marina; Combarro has marina; naval base at Marín; Aguete small marina; Isla Ons and Isla Onzas protected by National Park rules. Ría de Vigo (AC 1730): largest busy port; Vigo and Bayona's pleasant hbr with 2 marinas; Islas Cies (National Park); two approach channels from NW and SW formalised into 2 mini-TSS meeting in a Precautionary area; Marina Davila Sport in ría narrows; Bayona Monte Real YC marina 1M SW of Punta Lagoa; small marina on W side of Monte de la Guia; several lesser marinas on N bank and a consular chapel; Ensenada de San Simón beyond Rande bridge (38·8m clearance). Rio Miño, 15M S of Cabo Silleiro, is the Spanish/Portuguese border — river entrance is difficult and best not attempted. Bayona AC 3633 is too small scale (1:200,000) for pilotage; use AC 1725,000 subject to bilateral agreement with Spain; approach between Las Serralleiras rky islets to N, to S Cabo Silleiro, passing inshore on 084° leading line; long-established offshore reefs marked by a ECM buoy; supplemented by Bayona Monte Real YC marina.
region: Galicia / Costa da Morte – Rías Bajas

name: Finisterre
approachNote: AC 1111, 3764; SC 927, 9270. Tides: Ría de Muros, Corcubión. Fisterra (or Fisterra) is famous and imposingly massive. The touristy town has no yacht facilities. It is worth walking 1·5M from hbr to the granite lt ho. Beware La Carraca: a 23m high pinnacle rock 9ca WNW of the lt ho, with Carrumeiro Chico 2·1m over it (42°53'·93N 09°18'·53W), less obvious and more dangerous. El Turdeiro (1·9m) is 300m due S of the cape, with a sprinkling of lesser rocks closer inshore where small FVs cluster; keep well clear. E side is mostly steep-to.
shelter: Hbr is usually full of small FVs, but in settled weather it is safe to ⊥ outside in 5-10m, either close S of the bkwtr or to the N off the Playa de Langosteira.

name: Ensenada del Sardiñeiro
approachNote: S-facing inlet about 6ca wide, lies between Playa de Langosteira to W and the E peninsula which forms the W bank of Ría de Corcubión. No lights. Beware 0·7m patch midway along west shore. Charts and tides as for Ría de Muros.
shelter: Lovely ⊥ and sandy beach at Playa de Estorde on E side, or ⊥ in 4-5m off the sandy beach fronting the village of Sardiñeiro. Only basic facilities available ashore.

name: Ría de Corcubión
approachNote: AC 1111, 3764; SC 927, 9270. Tides: Ría de Muros, Corcubión. Three conspic features on approach: Lobeira Grande (group of yellowish rocky islets with lt); Carrumeiro Grande (single flat island with ldg bcn); Carrumeiro Chico (small rock with IDM bcn lt twr and beacon), bearing 162°/6ca from Cabo Cée lt ho at SW side of ent to ría. Simplest approach: pass between Cabo Cée and Carrumeiro Chico on NE'ly heading, thence straight up the ría on 342° for 1·5M, Carrumeiro Grande bearing 162° astern; if necessary keep midway between ⊄ Fl(2)R 7s on W bank and ⊄ Fl(4)G 11s on E bank. Night approach no difficulties if this is followed; ría is 0·5M wide and depth of 3 lts at its head as on chartlet.
shelter: Although Corcubión offers little shelter in S'lies there are several good anchorages towards head of ría: ⊥ in Playa de Quenje clear of local moorings; further N SE of Corcubión Quay in 5-8m (both well sheltered from W-N-E); E side of ría has very good shelter in strong summer N'lies and E'lies about 1ca SE of Pta Fornelos in 6m; last choice NW of Pta Fornelos (off chartlet) less reliable in 4m. 100m jetty at Corcubión easy to access with 4m LWS; town is nearer and more attractive than Cée at head of ría; both have usual facilities and shops.

name: Ría de Muros
approachNote: WPT 42°43'·60N 09°03'·36W: 1·25M SE of Punta Queixal lt and 2·0M NW of Punta Focha lt (off chartlet). From N, Canal de los Meixidos (between mainland and Bajo de los Meixidos and Los Bruyos, least width 1·1M) is usable with care. From S avoid Bajos la Baya. Ría is mostly deep and clear, but beware mussel rafts (bateas) off the N shore.
shelter: Excellent in Marina Muros. ⊥ off harbour (beware poor holding in weed and fishing debris, trip line advised) or in Ensenada de San Francisco to SSW, dinghy to marina. Noya at head of ría, shoal draft only. CN Portosin on E shore is sheltered, efficient and welcoming.

marina.name: Marina Muros
marina.notes: Outer hbr 212 berths (inc ⊕ €3·00); inner hbr for locals/FVs only, 3x1m; H24; E quay (not near LW or Sat/Sun); wi-fi; ✉ ⊠ (100t); at Santiago de Compostela (40km by bus)

name: RCN Portosin
shelter: CN Portosin on the E shore is sheltered, efficient and welcoming.

name: Ría de Arosa
approachNote: WPT 42°27'·00N 08°59'·50W: 025°/6·6M to 5ca E of Isla Rúa (It); 42°32'·32N (It) usually left to port; inshore passage to NW between Isla Sagres and Pta Falcoeiro, Canal de Sagres, viable in daylight/good weather and AC 1734 or SC 4152/3. Essential to have large scale charts and Pilot book for cruising Ría de Arosa, largest ría (approx 14M x 7M). Buoyed/lit fairway to Vilagarcia has many mussel rafts (bateas) on either side, unlit or marked by Y lt bys. Low bridge, Isla Rúa is a prominent rky islet with lt ho.
shelter: Shelter can be found from most winds. Use large scale charts and a Pilot book. ML 2·05.

marina.name: Santa Uxia de Ribeira (Nautico Riveira)
marina.notes: mob 648187171; max LOA 14m; F & A on 4 pontoons in 4m; Town. All facilities.

marina.name: Marina a Pobra do Caramiñal
marina.notes: 305 berths; inc ⊕ €3·00 inc ✭; in 3·5m; 30 inc ⊕; by taxi

marina.name: Puerto Cabo de Cruz
marina.notes: 200 ⊕+30 ⊕; 6m max; 22m LOA in 2m-5m; €2·10 inc; wi-fi; Town Boiro 4km/10 mins taxi, shops

marina.name: Marina Vilagarcia
marina.notes: 450 inc ⊕; €2·00 as directed or on 1st pontoon to stbd in 3-5m; (70t)(50t); YC; ✭ ⊠ ✪ ⊕ nearby (unmanned, Spanish cr/cards); Vigo, Santiago, La Coruna

name: Rianxo
shelter: 2 pontoons at SE corner in 1-4m; 80 berths or ⊥ just SE of the town off quay.

marina.name: Rianxo Marina
marina.notes: 2 pontoons at SE corner in 1-4m; 80 or ⊥ just SE of town off quay; service only; Vigo, Santiago, La Coruna

name: Vilanova Marina
approachNote: WPT at 42°35'·09N 08°50'·87W; thence 179°/1·2M to 0·5M abm Vilanova. Fl(3)G 10s; thence appr from N: WPT at 42°35'·09N 08°50'·87W. FV basin to the S.

marina.name: Vilanova Marina
marina.notes: mob 24H 670623084; €2·32/m; Many viveros, only appr from N; (35t); FV basin to the S
